name: ZX Spectrum
description: Sinclair's 8-bit home computer platform, central to British computer gaming and known for inexpensive hardware, cassette software, and a huge bedroom-coder scene.

eras.title: 48K Era
eras.description: Early Spectrum period defined by inexpensive cassette games, bedroom programmers, and foundational British microcomputer releases.

eras.title: 128K Era
eras.description: Later period with expanded memory, improved sound, stronger arcade conversions, and more ambitious late Spectrum games.
